title: Strengthening Traceability Systems to Manage Salmonella Foodborne Outbreaks
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Salmonella     remains     a     leading     cause     of     foodborne     illness     worldwide  ,   with     outbreaks     causing     significant     public     health     and     economic     burdens  .   Effective     traceability     systems   are   essential     for     rapid     identification     of     contamination     sources  ,   reducing     the     impact     of     outbreaks  .   Current     systems     often     face     gaps   in data   integration  ,   interoperability  ,   and     timely     reporting  ,   which     delay     containment     efforts  .   Strengthening     traceability     involves     adopting   digital   technologies  ,   harmonizing   data   standards  ,   and     ensuring     end-to-end     visibility     from     farm     to     fork  .   This     approach     supports     timely     recalls  ,   minimizes     product     losses  ,   and     protects     consumer     health  .   Collaborative     action     between     regulators  ,   industry  ,   and     technology     providers     is     critical     to     develop     scalable  ,   cost-effective     solutions  .   Improved     traceability     ultimately     enhances     trust   in   the     food     system     and     safeguards     national     food     security  .
